title: Does physical feedback (pd) lift scores?
hypothesis: Agents score low because the run is an open loop — they can't read the chip or diff attempts — not because they can't reason. If so, the legible pd representation should lift scores.
note: PREVIEW — illustrative numbers until the gated live run. Control is anchored to the real codex result on the leaderboard (score 3.37, valid 1); treatment values are synthetic.

control.label: control · raw DEF (open loop)
control.blurb: Today's environment: the agent gets the floorplan DEF and grades once, blind.

trace.note: final DEF only — no pd history to inspect

treatment.label: treatment · pd (closed loop)
treatment.blurb: Same task + the legible, versioned .pd repo (pd view / status / diff) + the instruction overlay.

trace.note: pd commit history the agent could read + diff while iterating
